human_verification.notes: Reviewed the pack for scope-boundary specificity, family communication evidence handling, tenant adaptation assumptions, escalation behavior, and scenario coverage across in-scope and exception cases.

specialist_owned_evidence: Specialty boundary is explicitly limited to family communication support for elder and disability services with explicit refusal and routing boundaries.
Authoritative sources are public and mapped to workflow stages, with refresh/decay posture and review cadence.
Research summary covers required cross-industry workflow families, systems, artifacts, decision boundaries, and failure modes.
Functionality map and results enforce coverage of all required shared, workflow, and industry-profile abilities.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in this package pass set.
The specialist remains non-clinical and non-legal, with explicit escalation to adjacent owners for unsupported authority asks.

notes: The pack is validated as a specification-based artifact with explicit boundaries and readiness to escalate to tenant systems or adjacent specialists.
Materialization rules require tenant retrieval of care-plan and communication policy configuration before activation in runtime.
